title: [macOS Sequoia][applehv] vfkit crashes (trace trap) and Podman machine fails with VZErrorDomain Code=1
body:
### Issue Description

On macOS Sequoia (26.2, arm64) with Podman 5.7.1, using the applehv provider, podman machine start consistently fails. The VM image is created successfully, but vfkit crashes when starting the VM. The Podman CLI reports that the VM failed with a macOS Virtualization.framework error (VZErrorDomain Code=1).

Additionally, running vfkit directly with a minimal configuration also crashes with trace trap, which suggests an incompatibility or bug in vfkit on this macOS version rather than a simple configuration issue.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to Reproduce
Ensure no existing machines and clean up state:
bash

podman machine stop podman-machine-default || true
podman machine rm -f podman-machine-default || true

rm -rf /Users/$USER/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv
rm -rf /Users/$USER/.config/containers/podman/machine/applehv

podman system connection rm podman-machine-default || true
podman system connection rm podman-machine-default-root || true
Initialize a new machine (letting Podman choose the default image):
bash

podman machine init --cpus 2 --memory 4096 --disk-size 40
This completes successfully and creates:
bash

ls -lh /Users/$USER/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv
total ...
-rw-r--r--  1 <user>  staff    40G  podman-machine-default-amd64.raw
-rw-------  1 <user>  staff   128K efi-bl-podman-machine-default
Start the machine with debug logging:
bash

podman --log-level=debug machine start


### Describe the results you received

podman machine start fails immediately with:
text

Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
...
Error: Error Domain=VZErrorDomain Code=1 Description="Internal Virtualization error. The virtual machine failed to start." UserInfo={
    NSLocalizedFailure = "Internal Virtualization error.";
    NSLocalizedFailureReason = "The virtual machine failed to start.";
}
Error: vfkit exited unexpectedly with exit code 1
The debug log shows vfkit being invoked with what appears to be a valid configuration, for example:
text


helper binary path is: /opt/podman/bin/vfkit
helper command-line: [/opt/podman/bin/vfkit --cpus 2 --memory 4096 --bootloader efi,variable-store=/Users/<user>/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv/efi-bl-podman-machine-default,create --device virtio-blk,path=/Users/<user>/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv/podman-machine-default-amd64.raw --device virtio-rng --device virtio-vsock,port=1025,socketURL=/var/folders/.../podman-machine-default.sock,listen --device virtio-serial,logFilePath=/var/folders/.../podman-machine-default.log --device virtio-net,unixSocketPath=/var/folders/.../podman-machine-default-gvproxy.sock,mac=5a:94:ef:e4:0c:ee --device virtio-fs,sharedDir=/Users,... --device virtio-fs,sharedDir=/private,... --device virtio-fs,sharedDir=/var/folders,... --restful-uri tcp://localhost:<port> --device virtio-gpu,width=800,height=600 --device virtio-input,pointing --device virtio-input,keyboard --gui --device virtio-vsock,port=1024,socketURL=/Users/<user>/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv/podman-machine-default-ignition.sock,listen]
...
Error Domain=VZErrorDomain Code=1 Description="Internal Virtualization error. The virtual machine failed to start."
Error: vfkit exited unexpectedly with exit code 1
The VM disk (podman-machine-default-amd64.raw) has the expected size (40G) and is present; EFI variable store is present as well.

### Describe the results you expected

podman machine start launches the VM via vfkit using the applehv provider, the VM boots successfully, and podman info works.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.

Environment
OS: macOS Sequoia 26.2 (25C56)
bash


Copy Code
sw_vers
ProductName:         macOS
ProductVersion:	26.2
BuildVersion:		25C56
Architecture:
bash

uname -m
arm64
Podman version:
bash

podman --version
podman version 5.7.1
vfkit version:
bash

/opt/podman/bin/vfkit -v
vfkit version: v0.6.1
Machine provider: applehv (default on macOS with Podman 5.7.1)
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details




### Additional information

Additional Minimal Reproduction (vfkit Alone)
Running vfkit directly, outside of Podman, with a minimal configuration also fails. This suggests a vfkit/macOS compatibility issue, not just a Podman configuration problem.

Commands:

bash

mkdir -p /tmp/vfkit-test
truncate -s 1G /tmp/vfkit-test/dummy.raw
touch /tmp/vfkit-test/efi-store

/opt/podman/bin/vfkit \
  --cpus 1 \
  --memory 1024 \
  --bootloader efi,variable-store=/tmp/vfkit-test/efi-store \
  --device virtio-blk,path=/tmp/vfkit-test/dummy.raw
Output:
INFO[0000] &{1 1024    {[efi variable-store=/tmp/vfkit-test/efi-store] true}  [virtio-blk,path=/tmp/vfkit-test/dummy.raw] none://  false  {[] false}} 
INFO[0000] boot parameters: &{EFIVariableStorePath:/tmp/vfkit-test/efi-store CreateVariableStore:false} 
INFO[0000]                                              
INFO[0000] virtual machine parameters:                  
INFO[0000] 	vCPUs: 1                                    
INFO[0000] 	memory: 1024 MiB                            
INFO[0000]                                              
INFO[0000] Adding virtio-blk device (imagePath: /tmp/vfkit-test/dummy.raw) 
zsh: trace trap  /opt/podman/bin/vfkit --cpus 1 --memory 1024 --bootloader efi,variable-store=/tmp/vfkit-test/efi-store --device virtio-blk,path=/tmp/vfkit-test/dummy.raw
So vfkit itself appears to crash (trace trap) when attempting to start the VM, even with a tiny disk and simple configuration.

What I’ve Already Tried
Completely removed and recreated the Podman machine:
podman machine rm -f ...
Deleting ~/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/applehv and ~/.config/containers/podman/machine/applehv.
Removing podman system connection entries.
Reinitialized the machine with different resources:
--cpus 2, --memory 4096, --disk-size 40.
Verified the VM disk and EFI files exist and are the expected sizes.
Confirmed that the error is not a simple typo or missing local image (using podman machine init without --image, letting Podman handle the default image).
Confirmed that the problem persists after a full cleanup and fresh podman machine init.
Hypothesis / Request
It appears that:

On macOS Sequoia (26.2, arm64),
with Podman 5.7.1 and vfkit v0.6.1,
vfkit is crashing when trying to start a VM via the macOS Virtualization.framework, resulting in VZErrorDomain Code=1 from Podman’s perspective.
Could you please:

Confirm whether this is a known issue with vfkit / applehv on macOS Sequoia?
Suggest any workaround (e.g. different vfkit version, different provider, specific flags) that would make podman machine usable on this OS?
If needed, I can provide any additional debug logs or run a custom vfkit build to help narrow down the crash.

Thank you

comments.body:
>  The VM disk (podman-machine-default-amd64.raw) has the expected size (40G) and is present; EFI variable store is present as well.

how did you install podman? It looks like you installed the amd64 which is not really compatible with arm64.
I would expect if you install podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg from the release page and re-create the machine to download the right image it should work
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
